{{Quote|Killed one of our operatives during a raid in the entertainment district.|The brief in Tibekk's bounty posting|<ref name="SWBH" />}}
'''Raim Tibekk''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Police officer/Legends|police officer]] living on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. He served in the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Police Force]] during the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Biography==
Raim Tibekk was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. He became a [[Police officer/Legends|police officer]] and joined the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Police Force]]. At one point, Tibekk was involved in a [[Raid in the Entertainment District|raid]] in the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Entertainment District]] against the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]]. During the raid, Tibekk killed a Cartel [[Unidentified Hutt Cartel operative|operative]]. In response, the Hutt Cartel placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for Tibekk's death in the amount of 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]].<ref name="BHPOSG" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] found Tibekk in Coruscant's [[Financial District (Coruscant)/Legends|Financial District]]. Fett managed to shoot and kill Tibekk and collected on the bounty.<ref name="SWBH" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Tibekk was a Human male with dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and light skin.<ref name="BHPOSG" /> When on duty, he wore his [[Coruscant Security Force uniform|CSF uniform]] and carried a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="SWBH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Raim Tibekk first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' a single player game released on [[December 5]], [[2002]]. The player can choose to pursue Tibekk's bounty though it is not required to move to the next level. He also had an entry in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' which was published on [[November 26]], [[2002]].
